concepts: compression release with retained intraday value

next_cycle_focus: Do not spend another cycle cosmetically simplifying the current SOL / 4h batch. Even after stripping away funding/compression/ADX ceremony, all three branches still failed at the walk-forward screen stage with zero trades, so the next exploration turn should rotate to a different supported lane or define an even more primitive always-on state rather than another SOL 4h continuation rewrite.
rationale: Cycle 153 is a clean exploration batch failure with the cycle_id kept exactly at 153. The current-cycle SOL / 4h continuous trend-state pullback, value reacceptance after failed breakdown, and compression-release higher-value acceptance branches all produced zero trades, grade F, and screen_fail outcomes. The genuine lesson is that removing ceremony from the old SOL 4h grammar still was not enough to make these continuation states live; the state definitions themselves remain too selective in practice. Because this was an exploration batch and all three current-cycle families failed identically without any evidence of edge, the correct action is to abandon only those three Cycle 153 families rather than pretend one deserves further refinement.
